column2: A user wants an LCD display with better control of dark areas than a conventional LED-backlit LCD. Which display technology should the technician consider?
column4:
Why

Mini LED is still an LCD backlight design, but it uses many smaller LEDs so lighting can be controlled in smaller zones.

Exam Trap

Mini LED improves backlight control; it is not the same as OLED, where the pixels emit their own light.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield MiniLED DisplayPanels Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A designer needs an LCD panel type known for strong color representation. Which LCD technology is the best fit?
column4:
Why

IPS panels are associated with excellent color representation, which makes them a better fit for color-sensitive work than a choice based only on response time.

Exam Trap

TN can be attractive for fast response, but the scenario asks for color representation.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield IPS Color Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A low-cost gaming display prioritizes fast pixel response, but users notice color shifts when viewing from the side. Which LCD technology matches that tradeoff?
column4:
Why

TN panels are associated with fast response times, while weaker viewing angles and color shift are the expected tradeoffs.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic TN DisplayPanels Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A hybrid laptop must support precise pen-style sketching and annotation. Which display input component matters most for that requirement?
column4:
Why

A touchscreen accepts direct touch input, while a digitizer supports stylus or pen-like input for more precise work.

Exam Trap

Do not treat every touch-capable display as equivalent when the requirement specifically calls for pen precision.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic Touchscreen Digitizer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A laptop LCD appears black, but a faint desktop image is visible when a flashlight is held close to the screen. Which display subsystem should be checked first?
column3: The backlight path, including the inverter if that model uses one.
column4:
Why

A faint image means the panel may still be producing the picture, but the lighting behind the LCD is not making it visible.

Exam Trap

Do not jump directly to replacing the graphics adapter when the image is present but not lit.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield Backlight Inverter Troubleshooting Source::Messer-v170

column2: A user wants smoother motion for fast games and sports video. Which display attribute should the technician prioritize, assuming the graphics hardware and cable also support it?
column4:
Why

Refresh rate describes how often the image can update. Fast-moving content benefits when the display, graphics adapter, and connection all support the higher mode.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield RefreshRate DisplayAttributes Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Two displays use the same resolution, but one is physically smaller. Which attribute explains why the smaller display can look sharper at the same viewing distance?
column4:
Why

Pixel density relates the pixel count to physical size. The same number of pixels packed into less space produces more pixels per inch.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.21-22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic PixelDensity Resolution Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A photo editor needs a display that can represent a wider range of colors for the target workflow. Which display attribute should be compared?
column4:
Why

Color gamut describes the range of colors a display can represent. For color-sensitive work, gamut support is more relevant than choosing by screen size alone.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.22; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield ColorGamut DisplayAttributes Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A mobile device display is thin and does not need a separate backlight because its pixels emit light directly. Which display type is this?
column4:
Why

OLED uses organic light-emitting material at the pixel level, so the panel does not depend on a separate LCD backlight.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.21; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 3.1
column9: A+::220-1201::3.1 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::DisplayTypesandAttributes Basic HighYield OLED DisplayPanels Scenario Source::Messer-v170
